title: Cardiovascular Protective Stack
blurb: For the heart-disease-runs-in-the-family situation.

title: Cognitive Longevity Stack
blurb: For brain aging and dementia risk reduction.

title: The Foundation Stack
blurb: Foundational only when your context supports it.

title: Metabolic Reset Stack
blurb: For pre-diabetes, fatty liver, and excess weight.

title: Over-50 Strength Stack
blurb: The anti-sarcopenia protocol — independence at 80 starts now.

title: Compare protocols
blurb: Pick 2–4 interventions and see them side by side.

title: Take the quest
blurb: Personalised stack from 8 short questions.

title: Blueberries and Anthocyanin-Rich Berries
blurb: Blueberries and other dark berries are a top dietary source of anthocyanins. Modest RCT evidence for blood pressure, vascular function, and cognition.

title: Brazil Nuts (Selenium)
blurb: Cheapest reliable selenium source. 1-2 nuts/day matches RDA. Don't exceed — selenium toxicity is real.

title: Calcium (Food First)
blurb: Important for bone, but more is not always better. Food-first calcium plus vitamin D and resistance training beats blind high-dose pills.

title: Coffee (Caffeinated)
blurb: Daily coffee (3-5 cups) is consistently associated with lower all-cause mortality across dozens of cohort studies. Causality probable but not proven.

title: Cold Exposure
blurb: Popular biohack with weak longevity evidence. Real for mood + brown adipose activation.

title: DASH Diet
blurb: The best-studied diet pattern for lowering blood pressure. Mediterranean-adjacent, but more explicit about sodium, potassium, and low-fat dairy.

title: Dark Chocolate (cocoa flavanols)
blurb: COSMOS trial (2022, n=21,442) is the first large RCT of cocoa flavanols on hard CV endpoints. 27% reduction in CV death.

title: Dietary Nitrate (Beetroot, Leafy Greens)
blurb: Dietary nitrate from leafy greens and beetroot is converted to nitric oxide. Modest BP reduction and exercise-performance benefit. Whole-food approach beats sup

title: Fermented Foods (yogurt, kimchi, kefir, miso, sauerkraut)
blurb: Stanford 2021 RCT: 6 servings/day for 10 weeks increased microbiome diversity AND reduced 19 inflammation markers. The strongest microbiome-modulating intervent

title: Flaxseed (ground)
blurb: Rodriguez-Leyva 2013 RCT: 30g/day reduced SBP by 10 mmHg in hypertensives over 6 months. Bigger BP effect than most foods.

title: Garlic (raw or aged)
blurb: Modest BP and lipid reductions in 30+ RCTs. Stiebeling 2024 added a 1500-person trial confirming the BP signal.

title: Ginger
blurb: Strong evidence for nausea (chemotherapy + pregnancy + motion). Mixed but real signal for HbA1c and inflammation markers.

title: Green Tea (drinking)
blurb: Multiple Japanese cohort studies link 3+ cups/day to lower CV and dementia mortality. Different from isolated EGCG capsules.

title: Higher Protein Intake (1.2-1.6 g/kg)
blurb: Protein needs rise with age, training, weight loss, and menopause. 1.2-1.6 g/kg/day is the practical longevity range for most adults over 50.

title: Legumes (beans, lentils, chickpeas)
blurb: Strongest single dietary predictor of longevity in Buettner's Blue Zone analyses. Real RCT data for HbA1c and LDL.

title: Low / No Alcohol
blurb: The 'small amount is good' story is dead. Less is consistently better.

title: Meditation / Stress Reduction
blurb: Modest effects on cardiovascular and inflammatory markers; subjective wellbeing real.

title: Mediterranean Diet
blurb: Strongest single dietary pattern for cardiovascular and all-cause mortality reduction.

title: No Smoking
blurb: The single largest modifiable mortality risk. Quitting at any age extends life.

title: Oat β-Glucan (Oatmeal, Oat Bran)
blurb: The cholesterol-lowering soluble fiber in oats. FDA-recognized health claim. Eat oatmeal — about as evidence-based as supplements get.

title: Oral Health
blurb: Periodontitis is independently associated with cardiovascular and Alzheimer's risk.

title: Pomegranate (juice or fruit)
blurb: Real human RCT data for blood pressure and carotid plaque. Strongest food-source polyphenol evidence outside olive oil.

title: Prolonged Fasting / Fasting-Mimicking Diet (FMD)
blurb: Periodic 3-5 day fasting cycles. Modest metabolic + immune renewal signals.

title: Resistance Training
blurb: Lean mass and strength are independent mortality predictors. Both decline without training.

title: Saturated Fat Replacement
blurb: The useful version is not “eat less fat.” Replace butter, cream, cheese, and fatty processed meat with unsaturated fats and high-fiber foods.

title: Sauna (Finnish-Style)
blurb: Strong dose-response cohort data on CV and all-cause mortality.

title: Sleep (7-9h, regularity, deep+REM)
blurb: Chronically <6h or >9h sleep both increase mortality. Regularity matters as much as duration.

title: Social Connection
blurb: Loneliness and social isolation increase mortality on the order of smoking 15 cigarettes/day.

title: Sodium / Potassium Balance
blurb: Lower sodium and higher food-based potassium reliably lower blood pressure. The effect is boring, measurable, and highly actionable.

title: Soy Foods (tofu, tempeh, edamame)
blurb: A useful protein-and-fiber food swap. Modestly lowers LDL and helps replace red/processed meat without losing protein.

title: Sun Exposure / Circadian Alignment
blurb: Daily morning sunlight anchors circadian rhythm. UV trade-off: balance D production with skin cancer risk.

title: Time-Restricted Eating
blurb: Compressed eating window. Modest metabolic benefit; mortality data thin.

title: Ultra-Processed Food Reduction
blurb: The most practical weight, glucose, and cardiometabolic nutrition rule: make packaged hyper-palatable food the exception, not the default.

title: VO2max Training
blurb: High-intensity intervals push the aerobic ceiling. Strongest single fitness-mortality lever.

title: Vaccines (flu, shingles, pneumococcal)
blurb: Annual flu vaccine reduces CV mortality 28% in recent MI patients (IAMI 2021). Shingles vaccine reduces dementia risk 20%. Among the most evidence-supported fou

title: Walnuts and Tree Nuts
blurb: Daily nut consumption is one of the strongest food-based mortality signals in epidemiology. PREDIMED-NUTS RCT showed ~28% CV-event reduction with ~30g/day mixed

title: Zone 2 Cardio
blurb: Sub-lactate-threshold steady-state cardio. The mitochondrial-density driver.

title: 17α-Estradiol (Men)
blurb: Stereoisomer of estradiol that extends male mouse lifespan without feminization. No human data.

title: ACE Inhibitors / ARBs
blurb: Hypertension treatment with mortality benefit and renal protection. Modest direct longevity signal.

title: Acarbose
blurb: Carbohydrate-blocker with strong mouse-lifespan signal. Limited human longevity data.

title: Dasatinib + Quercetin (D+Q Senolytic)
blurb: Mayo Clinic's pioneering senolytic combination. Promising mouse data, early human trials in specific diseases. NOT validated as a healthy-aging intervention. Do

title: Finasteride
blurb: Hair-loss drug with intriguing mortality signal in a large cohort study.

title: GLP-1 Agonists (Semaglutide, Tirzepatide)
blurb: Major weight-loss class with proven cardiovascular mortality reduction in obese patients.

title: Low-Dose Aspirin
blurb: Useful for secondary prevention. Net benefit in primary prevention is now disputed.

title: Menopausal Hormone Therapy (estrogen ± progesterone)
blurb: Started within 10 years of menopause, HRT reduces all-cause mortality ~30% and bone fractures substantially. Misunderstood for two decades after the WHI; the ti

title: Metformin
blurb: Diabetes drug with intriguing longevity signal in observational data. TAME trial in progress.

title: Rapamycin (Sirolimus)
blurb: The most consistent mammalian-lifespan-extending drug we have. Human longevity data still pending.

title: SGLT2 Inhibitors (Empagliflozin, Dapagliflozin)
blurb: Cardio-renal protection in diabetes, heart failure, and CKD — even without diabetes.

title: Statins
blurb: Single best-validated drug class for reducing cardiovascular mortality.

title: Testosterone Replacement (TRT) for hypogonadism
blurb: For men with confirmed hypogonadism (low T + symptoms): improves muscle mass, mood, sexual function. TRAVERSE 2023 NEJM established CV safety. Not for general '

title: Aged Garlic Extract
blurb: Garlic extract aged 18-20 months. Multiple meta-analyses show modest but consistent BP reduction. Safer than raw garlic at therapeutic doses.

title: Alpha-Lipoic Acid (ALA)
blurb: Modest evidence for diabetic neuropathy at high doses (600mg+). General-purpose 'antioxidant' marketing exceeds the actual longevity evidence. Consider only for

title: Ashwagandha
blurb: Adaptogenic herb (Withania somnifera) with consistent small-to-moderate RCT evidence for reducing stress and cortisol. Not a longevity drug.

title: Astaxanthin
blurb: Powerful carotenoid from microalgae. Modest evidence for skin elasticity, eye fatigue, and lipid oxidation. Marketed beyond what the data supports.

title: Berberine
blurb: 'Nature's metformin' — real glucose-lowering effect, no longevity outcome data.

title: CoQ10 / Ubiquinol
blurb: Mitochondrial coenzyme. Specific benefit in heart failure and statin myopathy; modest general signal.

title: Cocoa Flavanols
blurb: Plant compound from cocoa with the strongest RCT evidence of any 'food' supplement for heart-disease death reduction.

title: Collagen Peptides
blurb: Real evidence for skin and joint endpoints. No longevity outcome data.

title: Creatine
blurb: Most-studied performance supplement. Real benefit for muscle, modest cognitive signal.

title: Curcumin
blurb: Anti-inflammatory polyphenol with bioavailability problems and modest clinical effects.

title: EGCG (Green Tea)
blurb: Green tea consumption associates with lower mortality. EGCG-only pills have safety concerns.

title: Ergothioneine
blurb: Mushroom-derived amino acid with active uptake transporter. Strong mechanistic story; modest human data.

title: Extra-Virgin Olive Oil (Polyphenols)
blurb: The mortality data on EVOO consumption is among the strongest for any 'food'.

title: Fenugreek
blurb: Real RCT data for HbA1c reduction in T2D. Cheap, safe at typical doses. Modest effect compared to medications.

title: Fisetin
blurb: Senolytic flavonoid; clears senescent cells in mice. Human trials underway.

title: Folate (Vitamin B9)
blurb: Essential B-vitamin. Mandatory in pregnancy (prevents neural tube defects). Modest stroke-prevention evidence in folate-naive populations. Mostly handled by for

title: Glucosamine Sulfate
blurb: Originally a joint supplement, but two large observational studies (UK Biobank, NHANES) link daily use to ~15% lower all-cause and CV mortality.

title: GlyNAC (Glycine + N-Acetylcysteine)
blurb: Glutathione-restoring combo with surprisingly strong human pilot data in older adults.

title: Glycine
blurb: Cheap amino acid with small but consistent RCT evidence for improving sleep onset and quality.

title: Iodine
blurb: Essential thyroid nutrient. Boring if adequate, important if vegan/low-iodized-salt, risky at kelp-megadose levels.

title: L-Theanine
blurb: Amino acid found in green tea. Promotes calm focus without sedation. Modest evidence for stress and sleep quality, no effect on lifespan.

title: Lion's Mane Mushroom
blurb: Edible mushroom with two small human RCTs in mild cognitive impairment. Promising but underpowered.

title: Lithium (Low-Dose / Microdose)
blurb: Microdosing lithium has cohort data on suicide and dementia. Don't confuse with psychiatric doses.

title: Lutein + Zeaxanthin
blurb: Carotenoids that concentrate in the eye's macula. Strong RCT evidence for slowing age-related macular degeneration progression.

title: Magnesium
blurb: Common deficiency. Replete it. Repleted state is associated with lower mortality.

title: Melatonin
blurb: Sleep regulator with broader antioxidant + mitochondrial effects. Lower doses better than commonly sold.

title: N-Acetyl Cysteine (NAC)
blurb: Glutathione precursor with strong evidence for COPD exacerbations and acetaminophen overdose. Modest evidence for general antioxidant benefit.

title: NMN (Nicotinamide Mononucleotide)
blurb: Popular NAD+ precursor. Raises NAD+ levels in humans; longevity outcome unproven.

title: NR (Nicotinamide Riboside)
blurb: Older sibling of NMN. Same NAD+-raising effect, slightly different pharmacology.

title: Omega-3 (EPA/DHA)
blurb: Cardiovascular benefit at high pharmacological doses (icosapent ethyl). Mixed at supplement doses.

title: Plant Sterols / Stanols
blurb: Reliably lowers LDL by about 7-10%. Useful as an ApoB add-on, but not a substitute for statins when risk is high.

title: Probiotics (Specific Strain Matters)
blurb: Effect is strain-specific. Strong evidence for antibiotic-associated diarrhea and a few specific GI conditions. Marketing claims about general 'gut health' are

title: Psyllium Husk Fiber
blurb: Soluble fiber with FDA-recognized heart-disease risk reduction claim. Lowers LDL cholesterol and improves bowel function. Genuinely useful, often overlooked.

title: Pterostilbene
blurb: Methylated resveratrol analog with better bioavailability. Limited human longevity data.

title: Quercetin
blurb: Flavonoid commonly paired with dasatinib in senolytic protocols.

title: Red yeast rice
blurb: Lowers LDL cholesterol because it contains a low dose of lovastatin (a statin). Real effect, real risk, opaque dosing — most people would do better with a real

title: Resveratrol
blurb: Famous red-wine polyphenol. Hype outpaced evidence.

title: Spermidine
blurb: Polyamine that induces autophagy. Strong cohort mortality signal; cognition RCT results have been mixed.

title: Sulforaphane (Broccoli Sprouts)
blurb: Most potent natural Nrf2 activator. Mainly biomarker data; intriguing autism trial.

title: TMG (Trimethylglycine / Betaine)
blurb: Methyl donor often paired with NMN/NR to support methylation balance.

title: Taurine
blurb: Amino acid with strong 2023 mouse lifespan signal. Human longevity data uncertain.

title: Urolithin A
blurb: Postbiotic that induces mitophagy. Real human muscle-function data.

title: Vitamin B12
blurb: Essential vitamin. Deficiency is common in older adults and vegans, causes irreversible neuro damage. Supplementation only matters if you're deficient.

title: Vitamin C
blurb: Essential vitamin. Strong evidence for preventing scurvy. Modest evidence for cold-duration shortening. Megadoses do nothing extra.

title: Vitamin D3
blurb: Replete deficiency, yes. Supplement above sufficiency for longevity? Marginal.

title: Vitamin K2 (MK-7, MK-4)
blurb: Theoretical role in vascular calcification reduction. Strong cohort data, weak RCT data.

title: Whey Protein
blurb: The most-studied protein source for muscle synthesis. Most useful for older adults who under-eat protein and people who train.

title: Zinc
blurb: Essential mineral. Strong evidence for shortening colds when taken within 24h of symptoms. Long-term high doses cause copper deficiency.

What I'd actually take if I were 50
FDA expands tirzepatide label to include cardiovascular event reduction
blurb: Tirzepatide joins semaglutide on the short list of weight-loss drugs with a formal cardiovascular indication — meaningfully changing the insurance and prescribi

title: Why VO2max is the vital sign nobody measures (and how to measure yours)
blurb: Cardiorespiratory fitness predicts all-cause mortality more strongly than smoking, hypertension, and diabetes — and unlike those, your doctor probably hasn't me

title: Stop chasing rapamycin until you're sleeping 7.5 hours
blurb: The rapamycin discourse has gotten ahead of the data. The math says: fix sleep, train VO2max, eat Mediterranean — those have a 50–100× larger expected mortality

title: New trial readout — creatine and cognitive performance in older adults
blurb: A new 24-week RCT in adults 60+ shows modest but real improvements in working memory and processing speed with 5g/day creatine. Effect was concentrated in veget

title: The fiber protocol nobody runs — and why your gut ages faster without it
blurb: Most adults eat half the fiber the data supports. Fixing that is one of the highest-leverage, lowest-cost moves available — and it works through three independe

title: Zone 2 cardio: what it actually is, and why it isn't a fad
blurb: Zone 2 isn't a heart-rate gimmick — it's the specific intensity at which mitochondria are forced to adapt. Three sessions a week is the dose.

title: The protein RDA is wrong for adults over 50
blurb: The 0.8 g/kg/day RDA was set in 1968 for healthy young adults — and it's wrong for almost everyone over 50. The newer evidence-based target is 1.2–1.6 g/kg/day,

title: Vitamin D after VITAL: where the evidence actually lands
blurb: VITAL didn't kill vitamin D — it killed the idea that supplementing vitamin D in vitamin-D-replete adults would prevent cancer or cardiovascular events. The fra

title: Grip strength is a better mortality predictor than your blood pressure
blurb: A $20 hand dynamometer measures something more predictive of all-cause mortality than systolic BP, ApoB, or HbA1c. Most adults have never had it tested.
